commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch expands assistant reasoning-tag sanitization and embedded chat delivery scanners to strip `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks while preserving selected literal tag prose cases.

PR surface: Source +120, Tests +185. Total +305 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the remaining PR defect from source and review-thread evidence: an embedded block reply containing literal `Use </reasoning> to close the tag.` still reaches the separate `stripBlockTags()` close-tag path, which lacks the final sanitizer's new recovery. I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs author or maintainer follow-up for a narrow embedded block-reply regression and for redacted real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over while the external proof gate is still mock-only.

target.title: fix(agents): strip reasoning tags from chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

Addresses the final-sanitizer and embedded chat-delivery parts of #89473.

- Treat `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` as reasoning-control tags in assistant-visible final sanitization.
- Require an exact tag-name boundary so visible XML-ish prose such as `<reasoning-step>` stays visible.
- Keep leading and trailing unclosed `<reasoning>` bodies hidden, while preserving the concrete visible prose examples covered by review.
- Align embedded stream/block-reply scanners with the shared sanitizer grammar for attribute-bearing, self-closing, and narrow literal-prose reasoning tags.

## Root Cause

OpenAI-compatible providers can emit hidden reasoning inside `<reasoning>` blocks. The final assistant text sanitizer and embedded chat delivery scanners recognized older think/thinking/thought tag variants, but did not consistently recognize `reasoning` / `antml:reasoning` with the same boundary and tag-tail rules.

Two concrete delivery gaps are covered here:

1. Final/non-streamed assistant extraction could keep or truncate the wrong text around `<reasoning>` tags.
2. Embedded stream/block-reply delivery could detect a reasoning-looking chunk but fail to strip or recover it with the same rules as the shared sanitizer.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Final/non-streamed assistant text and embedded chat block replies now strip hidden `<reasoning>` / `<antml:reasoning>` blocks. Attribute-bearing reasoning open tags are stripped in embedded block replies. Self-closing reasoning tags do not hide following visible text. Visible non-control tags such as `<reasoning-step>` remain visible. Concrete visible prose mentions such as `Use <reasoning> tag for this example.`, `Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.`, and final/non-streamed `Use </reasoning> to close the tag.` remain visible where covered, while broader trailing unclosed bodies such as `tag for internal analysis`, `tags should be hidden`, `element should be hidden`, and `block should be hidden` stay hidden.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR worktree on this branch at head `cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9`, with GitHub Actions also green for that exact SHA. The embedded subscriber regression coverage exercises the chat-delivery/block-reply path that real channels use before outbound message delivery; it is the channel-comparable proof for this PR body.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran focused sanitizer, stream partitioner, OpenAI completions, embedded subscriber/block-reply regression tests, formatter check, diff whitespace check, and prompt snapshot check.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Earlier after-fix focused proof on the embedded delivery head also passed:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts
git diff --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
```

Evidence after fix: after-fix focused tests and embedded subscriber regressions show hidden reasoning content is stripped, visible XML-ish prose remains visible, attribute-bearing reasoning tags are handled, self-closing reasoning tags do not swallow following visible text, ordinary singular tag prose examples are preserved in final and embedded block-reply paths, and broader trailing unclosed `tag...` false positives are hidden.

Final/non-streamed sanitizer output covered by tests:

```json
{"input":"Before<reasoning>internal reasoning</reasoning>After","strip":"BeforeAfter","sanitize":"BeforeAfter","extract":"BeforeAfter"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> tag should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> tag for internal analysis","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <antml:reasoning> tag for internal analysis","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> tags should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> element should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> block should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example.","strip":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example.","sanitize":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example."}
{"input":"Use <reasoning> tag for this example.","strip":"Use <reasoning> tag for this example.","sanitize":"Use <reasoning> tag for this example."}
{"input":"Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.","strip":"Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.","sanitize":"Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example."}
{"input":"Use </reasoning> to close the tag.","strip":"Use </reasoning> to close the tag.","sanitize":"Use </reasoning> to close the tag."}
{"input":"Hidden reasoning </reasoning> final answer","strip":"final answer","sanitize":"final answer"}
{"input":"<reasoning> tag should be hidden","strip":"","sanitize":""}
{"input":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B","strip":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B","sanitize":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B","extract":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B"}
```

Embedded block-reply subscriber regressions:

```text
<reasoning hidden="1">Reasoning chunk that should not leak</reasoning>Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

<antml:reasoning hidden="1">Reasoning chunk that should not leak</antml:reasoning>Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

<reasoning/>Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

<antml:reasoning />Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

Use <reasoning> tag for this example.
=> block reply: Use <reasoning> tag for this example.

Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.
=> block reply: Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.

Visible prefix <reasoning> tag for internal analysis
=> block reply: Visible prefix

Visible prefix <antml:reasoning> tag for internal analysis
=> block reply: Visible prefix
```

GitHub Actions proof for latest head `cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9`:

```text
CodeQL Critical Quality run 27156195463 -> success
Workflow Sanity run 27156195454 -> success
OpenGrep PR Diff run 27156195543 -> success
CodeQL run 27156195505 -> success
CI run 27156195465 -> success
```

Observed result after fix: Closed reasoning blocks are removed from visible assistant text; concrete literal opening-tag examples survive final/non-stream extraction and embedded block delivery; the final/non-streamed literal close-tag example survives; leading and trailing unclosed reasoning text stay hidden; broader trailing unclosed `tag` / `tags` / `element` / `block` suffixes stay hidden; attribute-bearing reasoning tags are stripped from embedded block replies; self-closing reasoning tags do not swallow following visible text.

What was not tested: Full repository suite, live MiniMax/LiteLLM reproduction, and a separate manual live Discord/Telegram/Slack run. The proof above covers the final/non-streamed sanitizer, OpenAI-compatible stream partitioning through focused tests, and embedded chat block-reply delivery through the embedded subscriber regression harness.

## Verification

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts --reporter=dot
-> 2 files / 181 tests passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts
-> passed

git diff --check
-> passed

GitHub Actions for cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9
-> CodeQL Critical Quality, Workflow Sanity, OpenGrep PR Diff, CodeQL, and CI completed successfully
```

Earlier focused embedded delivery proof on this PR branch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts --reporter=dot
-> 5 files / 331 tests passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts
-> passed

git diff --check
-> passed

node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
-> Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)
```

target.body:
## Summary

Addresses the final-sanitizer and embedded chat-delivery parts of #89473.

- Treat `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` as reasoning-control tags in assistant-visible final sanitization.
- Require an exact tag-name boundary so visible XML-ish prose such as `<reasoning-step>` stays visible.
- Keep leading and trailing unclosed `<reasoning>` bodies hidden, while preserving the concrete visible prose examples covered by review.
- Align embedded stream/block-reply scanners with the shared sanitizer grammar for attribute-bearing, self-closing, and narrow literal-prose reasoning tags.

## Root Cause

OpenAI-compatible providers can emit hidden reasoning inside `<reasoning>` blocks. The final assistant text sanitizer and embedded chat delivery scanners recognized older think/thinking/thought tag variants, but did not consistently recognize `reasoning` / `antml:reasoning` with the same boundary and tag-tail rules.

Two concrete delivery gaps are covered here:

1. Final/non-streamed assistant extraction could keep or truncate the wrong text around `<reasoning>` tags.
2. Embedded stream/block-reply delivery could detect a reasoning-looking chunk but fail to strip or recover it with the same rules as the shared sanitizer.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Final/non-streamed assistant text and embedded chat block replies now strip hidden `<reasoning>` / `<antml:reasoning>` blocks. Attribute-bearing reasoning open tags are stripped in embedded block replies. Self-closing reasoning tags do not hide following visible text. Visible non-control tags such as `<reasoning-step>` remain visible. Concrete visible prose mentions such as `Use <reasoning> tag for this example.`, `Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.`, and final/non-streamed `Use </reasoning> to close the tag.` remain visible where covered, while broader trailing unclosed bodies such as `tag for internal analysis`, `tags should be hidden`, `element should be hidden`, and `block should be hidden` stay hidden.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR worktree on this branch at head `cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9`, with GitHub Actions also green for that exact SHA. The embedded subscriber regression coverage exercises the chat-delivery/block-reply path that real channels use before outbound message delivery; it is the channel-comparable proof for this PR body.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran focused sanitizer, stream partitioner, OpenAI completions, embedded subscriber/block-reply regression tests, formatter check, diff whitespace check, and prompt snapshot check.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Earlier after-fix focused proof on the embedded delivery head also passed:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts
git diff --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
```

Evidence after fix: after-fix focused tests and embedded subscriber regressions show hidden reasoning content is stripped, visible XML-ish prose remains visible, attribute-bearing reasoning tags are handled, self-closing reasoning tags do not swallow following visible text, ordinary singular tag prose examples are preserved in final and embedded block-reply paths, and broader trailing unclosed `tag...` false positives are hidden.

Final/non-streamed sanitizer output covered by tests:

```json
{"input":"Before<reasoning>internal reasoning</reasoning>After","strip":"BeforeAfter","sanitize":"BeforeAfter","extract":"BeforeAfter"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> tag should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> tag for internal analysis","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <antml:reasoning> tag for internal analysis","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> tags should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> element should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"Visible prefix <reasoning> block should be hidden","strip":"Visible prefix","sanitize":"Visible prefix"}
{"input":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example.","strip":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example.","sanitize":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example."}
{"input":"Use <reasoning> tag for this example.","strip":"Use <reasoning> tag for this example.","sanitize":"Use <reasoning> tag for this example."}
{"input":"Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.","strip":"Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.","sanitize":"Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example."}
{"input":"Use </reasoning> to close the tag.","strip":"Use </reasoning> to close the tag.","sanitize":"Use </reasoning> to close the tag."}
{"input":"Hidden reasoning </reasoning> final answer","strip":"final answer","sanitize":"final answer"}
{"input":"<reasoning> tag should be hidden","strip":"","sanitize":""}
{"input":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B","strip":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B","sanitize":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B","extract":"A <reasoning-step>visible</reasoning-step> B"}
```

Embedded block-reply subscriber regressions:

```text
<reasoning hidden="1">Reasoning chunk that should not leak</reasoning>Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

<antml:reasoning hidden="1">Reasoning chunk that should not leak</antml:reasoning>Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

<reasoning/>Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

<antml:reasoning />Final answer
=> block reply: Final answer

Use <reasoning> tag for this example.
=> block reply: Use <reasoning> tag for this example.

Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.
=> block reply: Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.

Visible prefix <reasoning> tag for internal analysis
=> block reply: Visible prefix

Visible prefix <antml:reasoning> tag for internal analysis
=> block reply: Visible prefix
```

GitHub Actions proof for latest head `cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9`:

```text
CodeQL Critical Quality run 27156195463 -> success
Workflow Sanity run 27156195454 -> success
OpenGrep PR Diff run 27156195543 -> success
CodeQL run 27156195505 -> success
CI run 27156195465 -> success
```

Observed result after fix: Closed reasoning blocks are removed from visible assistant text; concrete literal opening-tag examples survive final/non-stream extraction and embedded block delivery; the final/non-streamed literal close-tag example survives; leading and trailing unclosed reasoning text stay hidden; broader trailing unclosed `tag` / `tags` / `element` / `block` suffixes stay hidden; attribute-bearing reasoning tags are stripped from embedded block replies; self-closing reasoning tags do not swallow following visible text.

What was not tested: Full repository suite, live MiniMax/LiteLLM reproduction, and a separate manual live Discord/Telegram/Slack run. The proof above covers the final/non-streamed sanitizer, OpenAI-compatible stream partitioning through focused tests, and embedded chat block-reply delivery through the embedded subscriber regression harness.

## Verification

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts --reporter=dot
-> 2 files / 181 tests passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts
-> passed

git diff --check
-> passed

GitHub Actions for cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9
-> CodeQL Critical Quality, Workflow Sanity, OpenGrep PR Diff, CodeQL, and CI completed successfully
```

Earlier focused embedded delivery proof on this PR branch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts --reporter=dot
-> 5 files / 331 tests passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts
-> passed

git diff --check
-> passed

node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
-> Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)
```

Visible non-control tags such as `<reasoning-step>` remain visible. Concrete visible prose mentions such as `Use <reasoning> tag for this example.`, `Use <antml:reasoning> tag for this example.`, and final/non-streamed `Use </reasoning> to close the tag.` remain visible where covered, while broader trailing unclosed bodies such as `tag for internal analysis`, `tags should be hidden`, `element should be hidden`, and `block should be hidden` stay hidden.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR worktree on this branch at head `cf77969f3cd69869bf0113efec6f3bbf0c54a2b9`, with GitHub Actions also green for that exact SHA. The embedded subscriber regression coverage exercises the chat-delivery/block-reply path that real channels use before outbound message delivery; it is the channel-comparable proof for this PR body.
